behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP applies: correctness depends on real AI vision classification quality for explicit content; deterministic stubs can hide real misses. REAL-INFRA is not the core boundary here, and MULTI-STEP does not apply because this behavior is a single model judgment plus deterministic hold/publish routing. Rejected Workflow based on behavior text: it only specifies one image moderation outcome, not a multi-agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Submit known explicit images to the real vision model path and verify each is marked flagged, stored in held-for-review state, and never reaches publish output.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP applies directly: detecting obfuscated hate speech is model robustness/quality, not just routing logic. A mocked classifier would likely always pass and hide true failures. Rejected Workflow because behavior text describes one text-classification decision, not multiple real agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Send a corpus of obfuscated hate-speech variants through the real text moderation model integration and assert they are flagged and held, including unicode-lookalike and substitution cases.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS applies at module boundaries: this failure appears when image-check result, text-check result, decision logic, and publish path are wired together. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess artifact in the behavior text; (b) no dependence on real AI output quality because pass/pass can be injected deterministically; (c) no multi-agentic chain required. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior is about local handoff and final state transition when both checks already pass.
behaviors.test_description: Wire moderation service + decision engine + persistence/publish modules with deterministic pass responses for both checks; assert the post is published exactly once, not queued/held, and not dropped from storage/queue.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Lower than Integration would miss cross-module drop/hold wiring failures.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA applies: end-to-end latency budget depends on real network/service timing and runtime behavior; in-process fakes can hide timeout/queueing/connection effects. LLM-DEP is secondary because correctness criterion is timing, not model judgment quality. Rejected Agentic/Workflow based on behavior text: it requires completion time of the pipeline, not quality of AI outputs or multi-agentic reasoning outcomes.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end submissions through deployed moderation components with real model endpoints and transport; measure submit-to-decision/publish latency and assert p95 (or specified percentile) is <=5s under representative peak-hour load.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local module interaction: classifier error handling -> retry queue -> approval gate. This can be caught by wiring modules with a failing model client stub. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) real OS/network is not strictly required to reproduce the contract failure; (b) no dependence on real AI output quality; (c) no multi-agentic sequence. Rejected System because behavior text focuses on fallback state transition ('queued for retry rather than auto-approved'), not environment-specific transport quirks.
behaviors.test_description: Integrate moderation service with retry queue and a vision client test double that returns unavailable/timeouts; assert post enters retry queue with retry metadata and is never marked approved/published.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP applies: borderline-image consistency risk is driven by real model variability; deterministic mocks can hide instability. This is not just a pure cache unit test because behavior explicitly targets borderline real-image outcomes across repeated submissions. Rejected Workflow because this is repeated evaluation of the same moderation decision, not a distinct multi-step agent pipeline with multiple dependent agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image repeatedly through the real vision moderation path within the configured time window and verify decision consistency policy (e.g., same held/publish outcome each time, via cache/sticky decision behavior).
